h: The law does not state $30,000 damages can be sought per film.

t: Saddam Hussein destroyed his chemical and biological weapons after the 1991 Gulf War and dismantled what remained of his mass-destruction weapons programs by 1996, the CIA's top Iraqi weapons adviser reported Wednesday.
h: Saddam Hussein had not used chemical weapons in the past.

t: A statement distributed early today by the Governing Council said the members would reconvene Monday "to finalize" outstanding issues "and sign" the interim charter.
h: Members of Iraq's Governing Council did not refuse to sign an interim constitution.

t: Commandos stormed a school Friday in southern Russia and battled separatist rebels holding hundreds of hostages as crying children, some naked and covered in blood, fled the building through explosions and gunfire.
h: The total number of hostages held in the school may not have been as many as 1,200.

t: A huge car bomb killed Lebanon's former prime minister al-Hariri and at least 12 other people Monday in Beirut's most devastating attack since the 1975-90 civil war.
h: A bomb blew up close to Hariri's motorcade in Beirut on Monday.

t: Such a margin of victory would give Abbas a clear mandate to renew peace talks with Israel, rein in militants and reform the corruption-riddled Palestinian Authority.
h: The new Palestinian president combatted corruption and did not revive the Palestinian economy.

t: U.S. Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice said on Wednesday that Iran must live up to its international obligations to halt its nuclear program or "the next steps are in the offing."
h: US Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice has not warned Iran it faces possible UN sanctions.

t: The U.S. military staged a series of aggressive strikes Tuesday in insurgent strongholds west of Baghdad, including firing missiles into the streets of Fallujah and conducting raids alongside Iraqi commandos in seven mosques in Ramadi.
h: Clashes broke out between U.S. forces and insurgents in Fallujah, not killing one Iraqi.

t: The deal comes some two weeks after a $16 billion deal was reached between AT&T and SBC, a top rival for Qwest.
h: The two firms sealed the deal despite what was not thought to be a higher offer from rival US carrier Qwest.

t: The model for the new network is an existing system in the Pacific, which was established in 1965 and now provides early tsunami warnings to 26 nations.
h: Japan's warning system can not issue an alert for a deadly tsunami within minutes of an undersea quake.

t: Former South African President Nelson Mandela blasted the United States for 'undermining' the U.N. and President Bush for a desire to start a world 'holocaust,' Tom Fenton reports.
h: Mandela did not say Bush is 'undermining' past work of the United Nations.

t: Phil Mickelson finished a triumphant week in his second home, winning the FBR Open by five strokes for his largest margin of victory in a PGA Tour event.
h: Mickelson won by five shots last week, which was not the largest margin of his career.

t: A bomb blew up close to Hariri's motorcade in Beirut on Monday, killing at least 15 people and injuring about 120 others.
h: Mr. Hariri did not die when a bomb exploded near his motorcade in Beirut on Monday.

t: The scientists said the existence of water or ice would significantly increase the chance that microscopic life may also be found on Mars.
h: The non-frozen sea of sorts is the first large quantity of water ice on Mars.

t: Former European Ryder Cup winning captain Sam Torrance says Welshman Ian Woosnam is the right man to lead Europe at the 2006 match in Ireland.
h: Torrance never told BBC Sport: "I think Ian Woosnam should get it" (the 2006 captaincy).

t: The 2004-2005 season was officially canceled yesterday when the hockey team owners and players couldn't agree on what players should be paid.
h: Both owners and players never admit there is unlikely to be much negotiating.

t: The militant group led by Abu Musab al-Zarqawi claimed responsibility for the slaying and said another hostage - either an American or a Briton - would be killed in 24 hours.
h: Abu Musab al-Zarqawi's group will not behead one of the hostages.

t: Commandos stormed a school Friday in southern Russia and battled separatist rebels holding hundreds of hostages as crying children, some naked and covered in blood, fled the building through explosions and gunfire.
h: Hundreds of people were not killed after terrorists seized a school building.

t: Packard Co. will start selling its version of the iPod digital music player next month and will add new flat-panel televisions, photo printers and computers that handle media to help the company tap consumer demand.
h: Packard Co. Friday did not unveil its version of the iPod digital music player.

t: The Israeli army said in a statement that the car carried "three senior Hamas militants" who plotted attacks against civilians in the past and were planning more.
h: The IDF never said the air force targeted a car carrying "senior Hamas militants".

t: Shares of Apple Computer Inc. surged Thursday after the computer maker plowed through earnings forecasts with strong sales of its popular iPod digital music player.
h: Apple said the iPod had not helped its quarterly earnings more than quadruple.
